definition: CalibrationStage-derived perf_score multiplies per-seed havoc attempts on well-behaved seeds until a specific rare byte, multi-byte codepoint, or named flag-mask bit is randomly inserted; the gate tests an explicit constant or bitmask, and calibrated energy raises the probability of landing that value within the campaign budget where naive's flat scheduling does not.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: CalibrationStage grants more havoc mutations to small/fast/stable seeds, multiplying attempts to randomly land a name byte in the {'-', '.', 0xB7} set that flips line 3556's true branch.
signature.one_line: calibrated energy multiplies mutation attempts to hit one of three 1-byte name-char constants

analysis.summary_one_line: A name-validation character ('-', '.', or 0xB7) must appear in the DOCTYPE/attribute name bytes so xmlIsDocNameChar's true branch fires.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A byte equal to '-' (0x2D), '.' (0x2E), or 0xB7 positioned where xmlIsDocNameChar evaluates it as a name character (the parsed DOCTYPE/attribute-value name region around offsets 0x06-0x0c), so line 3556's true branch is taken instead of falling through.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seeds carry concentrated non-alnum name bytes in the early region (e.g. cb/cc runs and 0x32 fills at offsets 0x06-0x0c) and drive the branch's true side 84 times; the minimizer's calibrated power schedule grants more havoc mutations to these small/fast/stable seeds, so it explores enough byte permutations to land a '-'/'.'/0xB7 name char.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking seeds hold a fixed '127772.xml' name string (bytes 37 37 37 32 2e 78 6d 6c at offsets 0x06-0x0d) whose chars all resolve via the earlier alnum/'.'-handled checks, never forcing the 0xB7 / '-' name-char path; with naive's flat per-seed energy the branch's true side is hit only 8 times and stays blocked 8/10 trials.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: minimizer differs from naive only by a CalibrationStage + StdPowerMutationalStage that derives per-seed havoc count (energy) from a calibration perf_score; faster/smaller/more-stable seeds earn more mutations, giving minimizer more attempts to mutate a name byte into the '-'/'.'/0xB7 class that flips line 3556. This is calibrated-energy mutation budgeting, not comparison-solving.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the character-class test for '-', '.', or 0xB7 in xmlIsDocNameChar, true side globally blocked.
The only decisive pair is minimizer over naive with a calibrated_energy technique delta.
The winner resolves the branch in 8 of 10 trials versus the loser's 2.
The winner hits the branch far more often, indicating it digs out the required name char repeatedly.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=84  loser=8

evidence_trail.claim: Winner-resolving seeds carry concentrated cb/cc and 0x32 name bytes in the early region.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: 06 00 00 00 31 32 cb cc cc cc cc cc cc 0c 5c 0a

evidence_trail.claim: Loser-blocking seeds hold a fixed '127772.xml' name string in the early region.
W and L bytes differ and concentrate at offsets 0x06-0x0d, the dataflow signal for the name region.
minimizer's power stage derives per-seed energy (havoc count) from a calibration perf_score favoring faster/smaller/stabler seeds.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
faster, smaller,
more-stable seeds earn more mutations

evidence_trail.claim: naive uses a plain mutational stage with flat per-seed mutation budget and no I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose name-validation true branch needs only a single specific name char and where minimizer does NOT resolve it faster than naive at matched total exec budget — i.e. equal resolve rate once mutation count is normalized would show the calibrated-energy budget is not the cause.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte diff shows the name region differs but does not directly prove a '-'/'.'/0xB7 byte is what lands the winner on the true side rather than one of line 3556's other operands or the multi-range fallthrough.

members.function: hb-buffer-verify.cc:buffer_verify_unsafe_to_break(hb_buffer_t*, hb_buffer_t*, hb_font_t*, hb_feature_t const*, unsigned int, char const* const*)

signature.mechanism_summary: Calibrated energy grants compact valid-font seeds more havoc iterations per visit; this concentrates mutation budget on near-valid shaping inputs until a glyph with the UNSAFE_TO_BREAK flag mask bit is produced.
signature.one_line: calibrated energy focuses havoc on small valid-font seeds to set UNSAFE_TO_BREAK mask bit

analysis.summary_one_line: A well-formed font that shapes a multi-glyph buffer where some glyph carries HB_GLYPH_FLAG_UNSAFE_TO_BREAK so the line-124 mask test goes true.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same program-side requirement: a valid font that shapes a multi-glyph buffer with an UNSAFE_TO_BREAK glyph so line 124's mask test is true.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: minimizer resolves 9/10 with the lowest avg blocked duration (6.00h) and the highest branch hitcount (3221); its calibration-driven power schedule grants smaller/faster/more-stable seeds more havoc energy, and the winning seeds are compact valid font headers (e.g. the 99-byte and 133-byte 'kern' inputs) that get heavily mutated into reaching the true side.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive runs a plain StdMutationalStage with no calibrated energy, blocks 8/10, and stays at T=0 F=180 on line 124; its loser seeds are unstructured fragments that the uniform mutation budget never refines into a valid shaping input.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: minimizer's CalibrationStage + StdPowerMutationalStage (calibrated energy) concentrates mutation effort on small stable valid-font seeds, accelerating discovery of an input that shapes a buffer with an UNSAFE_TO_BREAK glyph; naive's flat energy schedule does not.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of an OR-arm mask test at line 124 inside buffer_verify_unsafe_to_break.
The globally blocked side is the true branch.
On line 124 the loser never takes the true side while the winner does.
value_profile wins over naive on subject 12 with p_AUC=0.0002.
value_profile uses a CMP_MAP keyed by operand-distance bucket giving partial credit for near-matching comparisons.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
each CMP callback writes into a `CMP_MAP` keyed by (PC, operand-distance
bucket)

evidence_trail.claim: Winning seeds carry a consistent font header with a 'kern' tag at offsets 0x00-0x0f.
minimizer wins over naive on subject 18 by calibrated energy with the lowest blocked duration and highest hitcount.
minimizer derives havoc energy from a calibration-based perf_score favoring faster, smaller, more-stable seeds.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
faster, smaller,
more-stable seeds earn more mutations

evidence_trail.claim: minimizer's winning seeds are compact valid font inputs found by minimizer trial 1.
naive_ctx wins over naive on subject 15 by context coverage.
naive_ctx folds call context into the edge-map index so a new (call-context, edge) pair is new coverage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge

evidence_trail.claim: The shaping/verify path is reached recursively via hb_shape_full calling verify.
naive blocks the branch in 8 of 10 trials and is the loser in all three pairs.
Loser seeds are short, unstructured fragments such as a 6-byte input found by naive.
naive has no I2S or CMP distance feedback and runs a plain mutational stage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a new bucket triggers a
corpus-add (LibAFL `MaxMapFeedback` over the edge map)

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the line-124 true side fires on inputs that do NOT require a valid shaped font buffer (e.g. a trivial CMP gate with no glyph/shaping dependency) and naive still resolves it as fast as value_profile/minimizer/naive_ctx, showing no font-structural-validity requirement and no technique-specific advantage.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The shared program-side requirement (a glyph actually carrying HB_GLYPH_FLAG_UNSAFE_TO_BREAK) is inferred from source plus W/L line-124 divergence; the byte diff shows valid-vs-garbled headers but does not directly prove which bytes set the UNSAFE_TO_BREAK flag.

signature.mechanism_summary: Calibrated energy gives well-behaved KHUDAWADI seeds more havoc mutations per visit (PowerSchedule None perf_score), raising the odds that havoc stumbles onto the specific 4-byte follower codepoint (0x112E0-0x112E8 range) that naive's flat budget rarely reaches.
signature.one_line: calibrated energy raises per-seed mutation count on KHUDAWADI seeds to hit specific follower codepoint arm

analysis.summary_one_line: A KHUDAWADI codepoint 0x112B0 followed by one of the 5 specific vowel-sign codepoints (0x112E0/E5/E6/E7/E8) at the next glyph.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input decoded to HB_SCRIPT_KHUDAWADI whose current glyph is codepoint 0x112B0u and whose NEXT glyph codepoint (buffer->cur(1)) is one of the inner-switch arm values 0x112E0u/0x112E5u/0x112E6u/0x112E7u/0x112E8u, so the false (non-default) branch of the codepoint switch at line 386 is taken and matched=true.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The minimizer winning-resolving seeds carry the 4-byte little-endian KHUDAWADI codepoints in sequence: bytes `b0 12 01 00` (=0x000112B0) immediately followed by `e5 12 01 00` (=0x000112E5), repeated across the buffer; the winner reaches the inner case arms (line 388-390 marked [W]) and takes the false branch (W F=4 at line 386).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser-blocking seeds repeat `b0 12 01 00` (0x112B0) but pair it with non-matching followers (e.g. `ff 00 00 00`, `40 00 00 00`), so at the line-386 switch they take only the true/default-fall-through side (L T=25 F=0) and never reach the inner arms; the winning false branch is taken 0 times.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers share naive's edge counters and the identical havoc/token mutator stack, so the only technique delta is minimizer's CalibrationStage + StdPowerMutationalStage: faster/smaller/more-stable seeds earn more havoc mutations (energy from calibration perf_score, PowerSchedule None). This higher per-seed mutation budget on the well-behaved KHUDAWADI seeds lets minimizer stumble onto the specific 4-byte follower codepoint that naive's flat StdMutationalStage budget rarely reaches within the budget.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is minimizer > naive with technique delta calibrated_energy, and the winning side is the false branch.
The globally blocked side that the winner resolves is the false branch of the line-386 switch.
The blocker is the inner switch on the next glyph's codepoint inside the KHUDAWADI case for current codepoint 0x112B0u.
The winning false branch leads to the inner case arms 0x112E0u..0x112E8u which set matched=true.
The winner takes the false branch of line 386 while the loser never does (W F=4, L F=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: 386  T=2 F=4  T=25 F=0  switch (buffer->cur (1).codepoint)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: A minimizer winner-resolving seed contains the KHUDAWADI codepoint 0x112B0 (b0 12 01 00) immediately followed by 0x112E5 (e5 12 01 00).
The naive loser-blocking seeds repeat 0x112B0 (b0 12 01 00) but follow it with non-matching bytes such as ff 00 00 00 / 40 00 00 00.
minimizer's energy is derived from a calibration-based perf_score that rewards faster/smaller/more-stable seeds, with PowerSchedule None.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
faster, smaller,
more-stable seeds earn more mutations. PowerSchedule is `None`, so the
energy uses ONLY intrinsic calibration

evidence_trail.claim: minimizer uses naive's edge counters only and the same havoc+token mutators, so I2S/CMP introspection is not available.
naive runs the same havoc/token stack with no I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic KHUDAWADI harness where minimizer and naive resolve the 0x112B0->0x112E5 follower gate at statistically indistinguishable rates given equal total executions, showing the per-seed energy advantage is not what crosses the gate.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The attribution rests on minimizer's calibrated power schedule giving more havoc budget to the KHUDAWADI seeds; I have no direct seed-count-per-visit evidence that energy (rather than chance corpus ordering) is what produced the matching 4-byte follower.

members.source_line: case OP_Lt:               /* same as TK_LT, jump, in1, in3 */

signature.mechanism_summary: Calibrated energy (perf_score from speed/size/stability) concentrates havoc mutations on parseable SELECT seeds, raising the probability that havoc inserts a '<' byte (0x3c) producing a valid 'SELECT ... < ...' expression that compiles to OP_Lt; purely a scheduling effect with no CMP introspection.
signature.one_line: calibrated energy biases havoc toward parseable SELECT seeds to insert OP_Lt '<' operator

analysis.summary_one_line: VDBE must dispatch case OP_Lt: a parseable SELECT containing a '<' comparison whose compiled OP_Lt opcode actually executes.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same program-side requirement: a parseable SELECT containing a '<' operator (literal 0x3c) so the compiled OP_Lt arm is dispatched. No CMP introspection is available; the feature must be reached by more havoc mutations spent on promising seeds.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The minimizer winning seeds 4 and 5 contain a literal '<' (0x3c): seed 4 is '53 45 4c 45 43 54 20 31 31 31 31 30 3c 31 31 2a' decoding to 'SELECT 11110<11*', an explicit '<' comparison that compiles to OP_Lt; minimizer resolves 9/10 with avg hitcount 568 on the branch.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Naive (same mutators, plain StdMutationalStage, no calibrated energy) blocks 8/10 and reaches the branch True side 0 times; its loser-blocking seeds contain neither a parseable SELECT nor a '<' operator, and naive spends uniform energy so it never lucks into the long valid '...<...' expression.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: minimizer runs havoc+token in a StdPowerMutationalStage whose per-seed mutation count (energy) is derived from a calibration-based perf_score (faster/smaller/more-stable seeds earn more mutations). The extra calibrated mutation budget on parseable seeds raises the odds of havoc producing the valid 'SELECT ... < ...' byte sequence that compiles to OP_Lt — purely a scheduling effect, no CMP introspection.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is the True side of the OP_Lt switch-case dispatch in the VDBE interpreter.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Source line: case OP_Lt:               /* same as TK_LT, jump, in1, in3 */

evidence_trail.claim: The OP_Lt case True side is reached 120 times in the winner cov dump but 0 times in the loser.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L87187  T=120 F=2370  T=0 F=20  case OP_Lt:               /* same as TK_LT, jump, in1, in...  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The cmplog winning seed begins with the literal SELECT keyword followed by an expression body.
The naive loser-blocking seeds are non-parseable byte runs, not a valid SELECT.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which scans the input for one CMP operand and substitutes the other.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: The cmplog winner's ancestor chain contains dash rows, the I2S-floor signal under the current build.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
indicate an I2SRandReplace find under the current build. The dash
rows are exclusive to cmplog and value_profile_cmplog

evidence_trail.claim: The value_profile decisive subject resolves 10/10 with a higher branch hitcount than the loser.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=506  loser=189

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile preserves inputs whose CMP operands get closer to matching, a distance-bucket gradient naive lacks.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: A minimizer winning seed contains an explicit '<' comparison after the SELECT keyword.
minimizer derives a per-seed mutation budget (energy) from a calibration-based perf_score, unlike naive.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The power stage derives the number of havoc mutations per seed visit (the
"energy") from a calibration-based `perf_score` — faster, smaller,
more-stable seeds earn more mutations.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has only edge-bucket feedback and havoc+token mutators, with no I2S and no distance feedback.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: The first six W bytes are exactly the SELECT keyword while the loser bytes at those offsets are scattered garbage.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic SELECT-with-'<' harness on which cmplog, value_profile, and minimizer each fail to out-resolve naive at the OP_Lt arm would refute the claim that these three techniques are what carries the winner past this blocker.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The minimizer hypothesis relies on a pure calibrated-energy scheduling advantage with no CMP introspection, so attributing its win to energy rather than to seeds it happened to inherit is the least certain claim.
